itineraries.description: Australia's harbor city combining iconic landmarks, beautiful beaches, and vibrant culture

activities.description: Start at the waterfront, walk along the harbor
Guided tour of the iconic sail-shaped building (1.5 hours)
Walk through the tranquil gardens via Mrs Macquarie's Chair
Lunch at historic pub or café
Australian wildlife with harbor views (3 hours)
Final evening stroll under lit Opera House

itineraries.tips: Get an Opal Card for all public transport. Book Opera House tours in advance. Start early to avoid crowds.

itineraries.description: Australia's cultural capital known for its art, coffee culture, and diverse neighborhoods

activities.description: Hosier Lane street art, Degraves Street, Hardware Lane
Trendy cafes in the hip suburb
Rooftop tapas with city views (book ahead)
Main hub with shops and cafes

itineraries.tips: Melbourne is the coffee capital - try laneway cafes. Use Myki card for transport. Free Tram Zone covers CBD.

itineraries.description: A bustling metropolis blending tradition and modernity, famous for cherry blossoms and rich culture

activities.description: Tokyo's oldest Buddhist temple - arrive early
Relax and visit Tokyo National Museum
Former geisha district with French influence
Dinner - mix of French and Japanese
Choose: Kamakura (beaches/temples), Nikko (shrines), or Kawagoe ( Edo-period)

itineraries.tips: Get a Suica or Pasmo IC card. Download station map app. Book popular restaurants in advance.

itineraries.description: Ancient capital with thousands of temples, traditional geisha districts, and serene gardens

activities.description: Famous wooden terrace with panoramic views
Thousands of torii gates - full hike 2-3 hours

itineraries.tips: Arrive at popular sites at opening time. Use IC card for all transport. Dress modestly for temples.

itineraries.description: Lively city known for stunning beaches, vibrant carnival, and iconic Christ the Redeemer

activities.description: Iconic statue atop Corcovado Mountain (book ahead)

itineraries.tips: Book Christ the Redeemer tickets in advance. Use Uber for convenience. Be aware of surroundings at night.

itineraries.description: The City of Light, famous for the Eiffel Tower, Louvre, and world-class cuisine

activities.description: Book 9 AM tickets - stunning stained glass
Climb to rooftop for panoramic views
Lunch - try falafel on Rue des Rosiers

itineraries.tips: Book Eiffel Tower and Louvre tickets 2-3 weeks ahead. Use Paris Museum Pass for multiple sites.

itineraries.description: The Big Apple, known for Times Square, Central Park, and iconic skyline

activities.description: Iconic stores and St. Patrick's Cathedral
Top of the Rock for views

itineraries.tips: Book Broadway tickets via TodayTix. Get MetroCard for subway. Pre-book skip-the-line attractions.

itineraries.description: The Eternal City, home to ancient ruins like the Colosseum and Vatican City
itineraries.tips: Book Colosseum and Vatican tickets 2-3 months ahead. Dress modestly (cover shoulders/knees) for churches.

itineraries.description: Unique city built on water, famous for canals, historic architecture, and romantic atmosphere
itineraries.tips: Buy ACTV vaporetto pass (24h €25, 72h €35). Book museum tickets in advance. Check acqua alta forecasts in winter.

itineraries.description: Cradle of the Renaissance, home to masterpieces of art and architecture
itineraries.tips: Book Uffizi and Galleria dell'Accademia 2-3 months ahead. Walk everywhere - Florence is compact.

itineraries.description: Vibrant Mediterranean city famous for Gaudí's modernist architecture and lively beach culture

activities.description: Dragon-like facade on Passeig de Gràcia
Basílica de Santa Maria del Mar

itineraries.tips: Book Sagrada Família and Park Güell 2-3 weeks ahead. Pickpocketing is common on La Rambla.

itineraries.description: Tropical paradise with forested volcanic mountains, iconic rice paddies, and vibrant arts scene

activities.description: Cliffside temple with Kecak dance at sunset

itineraries.tips: Rent a scooter for flexibility (IDR 70,000-95,000/day). Use biodegradable sunscreen at cenotes.

itineraries.description: Stunning volcanic island famous for whitewashed buildings, blue domes, and spectacular sunsets
itineraries.tips: Book sunset cruise in advance. Rent car or ATV for flexibility. Start hike early with water.

itineraries.description: Chaotic yet captivating city of golden temples, bustling markets, and incredible street food

activities.description: Pad thai at market food stalls
Myth Massage or The Oriental Spa

itineraries.tips: Use BTS Skytrain and MRT subway for fast transport. Grab (Thailand's Uber) for fair prices.

itineraries.description: The Pink City of Rajasthan, known for royal palaces, vibrant bazaars, and stunning architecture

activities.description: Opens 9:30 AM - four gates representing seasons
Johari Bazaar for textiles and gems

itineraries.tips: Use Uber/Ola for reliable taxis. Buy composite ticket (INR 1000) for most sites. Avoid elephant rides at Amber Fort.

itineraries.description: Vibrant city famous for medieval medina, colorful souks, and stunning riads

activities.description: Haggling essential - start at 1/3 asking price

itineraries.tips: Walking tours recommended for Medina. Hire a guide for souks. Download offline Google Maps.

itineraries.description: Stunning coastal city backed by Table Mountain, known for beaches, vineyards, and rich history

activities.description: Boulders Beach penguins + Cape of Good Hope

itineraries.tips: Uber is super cheap. Book Table Mountain 2-3 weeks ahead. Day 3 tours include transport.

itineraries.description: Glamorous French Riviera city with Mediterranean beaches, Belle Époque architecture, and vibrant Old Town

activities.description: Walk along the iconic waterfront promenade
Explore narrow colorful streets of Old Town
Try socca and local specialties at the market
Hike or bike for stunning views

itineraries.tips: All beaches are pebbly - bring water shoes. Use efficient tram system to get around.

itineraries.description: Hilly city famous for Victorian architecture, cable cars, Golden Gate Bridge, and vibrant culture

activities.description: Visit Vista Point for iconic photos
Return by ferry with bay views
Ferry to the famous prison (book ahead)
Dinner in oldest Chinatown in US
Little Italy - lunch at Caffe Trieste

itineraries.tips: No car needed - use BART, buses, and cable cars. Summer fog is common. Book Alcatraz weeks ahead.

itineraries.description: Vibrant coastal city with beautiful beaches, Art Deco architecture, and Latin American culture

activities.description: Calle Ocho - Cuban culture and dinner

itineraries.tips: Rent a car to access Everglades and Key Biscayne. Use free trolley in downtown. Best weather Nov-Dec.

itineraries.description: Spain's capital known for world-class museums, royal palaces, and vibrant tapas culture

activities.description: Largest royal palace in Western Europe

itineraries.tips: Eat on Spanish schedule - lunch 2-4 PM, dinner 9-11 PM. Free museum hours: Prado last 2 hours weekdays.

itineraries.description: Mountain city in northern Thailand known for ancient temples, vibrant night markets, and elephant sanctuaries

activities.description: Most revered temple in Chiang Mai
Try famous Khao Soi noodle curry
Hike Monk's Trail or drive to mountain temple
Market tour and Thai cooking (book ahead)
Bua Tong - climb up the waterfall

itineraries.tips: Avoid Feb-April (burning season, poor air quality). Book elephant sanctuary in advance. Use songthaews (red trucks).

itineraries.description: Ancient city with Acropolis, Parthenon, and rich Greek history combined with modern vibrancy

activities.description: Arrive at opening to avoid crowds
Cable car or hike for 360° views

itineraries.tips: Book Acropolis tickets online in advance. Most sites require timed entry. Avoid July-August heat.

itineraries.description: India's coastal paradise with pristine beaches, Portuguese heritage, and lively party scene

activities.description: Learn about Goan spices and lunch
Day trip to famous waterfall (monsoon season)

itineraries.tips: Rent a scooter (INR 300-500/day). Wednesday is Anjuna Market day. North Goa is party-focused, South Goa is relaxed.

itineraries.description: Adventure capital of the world set against dramatic mountains, crystal lakes, and thrilling activities

activities.description: Bungee jumping at the original site

itineraries.tips: Rent a car for Milford Sound and Arrowtown. Book adventure activities in advance. Weather changes quickly.
